title: How France's pre-deportation centers became an 'extension of the prison system'
subhead: The rate of deportation for undocumented foreigners held in administrative detention centers has never been so low, even as detention periods have grown longer and conditions have deteriorated.

title: Air France and Airbus found guilty on appeal of involuntary manslaughter in 2009 Rio-Paris crash
Macron backs repeal of France's colonial-era slavery decrees amid reparations debate
Trump tightens grip on Republican Party in US primaries
subhead: Candidates backed by Donald Trump for the Republican Party nomination in the November midterm elections benefit from the mobilization of the MAGA base, which remains loyal to the billionaire despite his declining popularity.

title: NGOs sue French state over failure to tackle 'forever chemicals'
subhead: NGOs and residents living near contaminated sites filed legal action against the state to remedy the 'ecological harm' caused by its 'culpable inaction.'

title: These winemakers believe melody makes better wine: 'Right after bottling, we play Beethoven on a loop'
subhead: In the vineyards, while fermenting or even during aging, some winemakers use various melodies that they believe stimulate the vines, protect them from disease or intensify the wine's aromas.

title: From Italy to Libya, the dehumanizing processing of migrants
subhead: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (4/4). Exploited, extorted and abused in Libya, exiles who survive the crossing are rendered invisible and sometimes fall into the hands of mafias linked to agricultural businesses. But 'what matters is that irregular arrivals decrease,' as an official in Rome put it.

'Team Musul': The French youths convicted of attempting to join IS in Mozambique
subhead: Six young French nationals were convicted in Paris for planning to migrate to Mozambique together and join the IS-affiliated jihadist group al-Shabab in Cabo Delgado province, where it has been active since 2017.

title: Gaza's vanished past lives on through photographs accidentally rediscovered in Marseille
subhead: Kegham Djeghalian, a photographer and Armenian genocide survivor, settled in Gaza in 1944. He documented the Palestinian territory through portraits and reports until he died in 1981. His grandson has chosen to exhibit his images of ordinary and joyful moments in Gaza, now devastated by two years of war.

title: In a tsunami of data leaks, French society remains vulnerable and authorities powerless
subhead: France is experiencing a record number of data breaches. Despite the damage caused by these hacks, the level of cybersecurity in businesses and government agencies remains inadequate.
